raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1111 seeks to address challenges that local educational agencies (LEAs) face in adopting zero-emission school buses by 2035.",
    "AB 1111 extends the deadline for adopting zero-emission school buses to 2045.",
    "Many rural school districts lack the resources, capacity, or infrastructure to meet state-mandated requirements for zero-emission school buses.",
    "A recent survey of superintendents found challenges in adopting zero-emission school bus fleets, including cost, increased maintenance, and lack of charging infrastructure.",
    "Existing electrical infrastructure may not support the new charging stations needed for zero-emission school buses.",
    "AB 1111 addresses the shortcomings in existing legislation that set an impractical timeline for electric vehicle adoption.",
    "Many school districts face significant barriers to the adoption of zero-emission school buses, including performance across different topography and climate conditions.",
    "Infrastructure and utility challenges, safety concerns, and cost are barriers that need to be addressed for electric vehicle adoption."
  ]
}
```
